label: React / TypeScript conventions
description: React component and hooks patterns for TypeScript apps.
value:
## React / TypeScript Conventions

- Use functional components with hooks — no class components in new code.
- Use TypeScript strict mode. Avoid `any`; prefer explicit types on props and return values.
- Co-locate related files: components, hooks, tests, and types.
- Use `useCallback` for callback stability and `useMemo` for expensive computations.
- Keep components focused on a single concern. Extract logic into custom hooks.
- Test components with React Testing Library, not shallow rendering.
- Run Prettier and ESLint before committing.

label: React testing patterns
description: Test guidance for React and component interactions.
value:
## React Testing Guidance

- Prefer user-centric assertions with React Testing Library.
- Mock external APIs with MSW or similar test utilities.
- Avoid implementation details in tests. Test behavior and accessibility.
- Verify asynchronous UI updates using `waitFor` or `findBy*`.
- Keep tests fast and deterministic.
